title: Artificial Intelligence Foundations
summary: A broad AI foundation spanning agents, search, knowledge representation, uncertainty, planning, machine learning, neural networks, language and perception, generative AI, evaluation and responsible risk management.

assumed_knowledge: Basic algebra, graphs, probability and descriptive statistics or assigned remediation
audience.target_capability: Frame an AI problem, select and implement a modest symbolic or learned approach, evaluate it against meaningful baselines and risks, and communicate warranted limitations.

includes: AI literacy, problem characteristics and agents
Generative AI, prompting, embeddings and retrieval augmentation
Metrics, robustness, fairness, privacy, explainability and human oversight
non_goals: NumPy or Pandas as full separate library courses

authoritative_sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks

runtime.language: Python for experiments; language-neutral mathematics and pseudocode for concepts
runtime.standard: Python 3.14 with pinned scikit-learn 1.9.x and reviewed model/API fixtures
runtime.execution_environment: Isolated notebooks or scripts with pinned datasets, seeded randomness, bounded compute and no autonomous external actions
notes: Course examples use small reviewed datasets and models, not live uncontrolled outputs.
Vendor API demonstrations are mocked by default and never expose learner keys.
Rapidly changing model-specific content requires source and version review before publication.

modules.title: AI problems and agents
modules.description: Define AI operationally and characterize environments, agents and limitations.

skills.title: AI, automation and intelligence claims
skills.description: Distinguish AI techniques from fixed automation and evaluate claims by capability and evidence rather than marketing.
outcomes: Classify example systems and state what evidence would support an intelligence claim.

skills.title: Problem and environment characteristics
skills.description: Classify observability, determinism, dynamics, discreteness, agents and uncertainty.
outcomes: Characterize an AI task and explain how its properties constrain solution choices.

skills.title: Agent, goal and utility models
skills.description: Relate perception, state, actions, goals, utility and rationality under bounded information.
outcomes: Specify a simple agent's percepts, actions, objective and performance measure.

skills.title: History, cycles and limits
skills.description: Recognize symbolic, statistical and neural traditions and recurring gaps between benchmark and general capability.
outcomes: Place major approach families in context and identify an overgeneralized historical claim.

modules.title: State-space and adversarial search
modules.description: Represent problems as state spaces and compare informed and uninformed exploration.

skills.title: States, actions, goals and costs
skills.description: Encode a problem as an implicit graph with successors, goal test and path cost.
outcomes: Design a state-space representation for a puzzle or grid task.

skills.title: Uninformed search
skills.description: Compare DFS, BFS, iterative deepening and uniform-cost search by completeness, optimality, time and space.
outcomes: Select, implement and instrument an uninformed search for a stated state space.

skills.title: Heuristics, greedy search and A*
skills.description: Design heuristic estimates and relate admissibility or consistency to optimality guarantees.
outcomes: Implement A* and compare path cost and nodes expanded under two heuristics.

skills.title: Minimax and alpha-beta
skills.description: Back up utility through alternating choices and prune branches that cannot affect the decision.
outcomes: Trace and implement bounded minimax with alpha-beta pruning for a small game.

modules.title: Knowledge representation and uncertainty
modules.description: Encode facts and uncertain relationships and reason within explicit assumptions.

skills.title: Symbols, predicates and rules
skills.description: Translate a bounded natural-language domain into facts, relations, rules and queries.
outcomes: Create and evaluate a small symbolic knowledge base while stating closed- or open-world assumptions.

skills.title: Logical inference and consistency
skills.description: Apply forward or backward rule reasoning and identify contradictions or unsupported conclusions.
outcomes: Trace an inference chain and distinguish entailed, contradicted and unknown statements.

skills.title: Conditional probability and Bayes rule
skills.description: Update belief from prior, likelihood and evidence while avoiding base-rate neglect.
outcomes: Compute and interpret a posterior probability for a diagnostic scenario.

skills.title: Probabilistic graphical models concept
skills.description: Read dependencies and conditional independence from a small Bayesian-network structure.
outcomes: Construct and reason over a small directed probabilistic model without claiming causal meaning automatically.

modules.title: Planning and decision making
modules.description: Choose action sequences or policies under constraints and uncertain outcomes.

skills.title: Actions, preconditions and effects
skills.description: Represent a planning domain and distinguish plan validity from plan quality.
outcomes: Encode and validate a small goal-directed planning problem.

skills.title: Constraint-satisfaction problems
skills.description: Model variables, domains and constraints and use propagation plus backtracking.
outcomes: Implement and instrument a small CSP solver with a variable or value heuristic.

skills.title: Utility and expected decisions
skills.description: Compare actions by outcome utility and probability while making value choices explicit.
outcomes: Compute an expected-utility choice and critique sensitivity to its assumptions.

skills.title: Sequential decision and reinforcement concepts
skills.description: Recognize state, action, reward, policy and exploration in a Markov decision process without treating reward as neutral.
outcomes: Specify a small sequential decision problem and identify reward-hacking risks.

modules.title: Data and experimental workflow
modules.description: Turn a question into defensible data, features, splits and baselines.

skills.title: Problem, target and success measure
skills.description: Specify prediction unit, target, decision context and cost of errors before selecting a model.
outcomes: Write a measurable ML problem statement with non-goals and baseline.

skills.title: Sampling, labels and data quality
skills.description: Inspect provenance, missingness, duplicates, measurement error, representation and label uncertainty.
outcomes: Produce a data-quality report and identify who or what is absent or mismeasured.

skills.title: Features and preprocessing
skills.description: Encode, scale or transform only using training-derived information and meaningful representations.
outcomes: Build and explain a preprocessing pipeline that handles numeric and categorical inputs.

skills.title: Train, validation, test and leakage
skills.description: Split according to deployment structure and keep test information outside training and tuning.
outcomes: Choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage.

modules.title: Supervised learning
modules.description: Fit and evaluate regression and classification models against meaningful baselines.

skills.title: Regression
skills.description: Model continuous targets and inspect residual patterns and scale-sensitive errors.
outcomes: Train and evaluate a baseline and linear regression model with justified metrics.

skills.title: Classification and probabilities
skills.description: Model categorical outcomes and distinguish class labels, scores and probability estimates.
outcomes: Train and evaluate a classifier under class imbalance and asymmetric error costs.

skills.title: Linear, neighbor and tree models
skills.description: Compare assumptions, scaling needs, nonlinear boundaries and interpretability of common model families.
outcomes: Select and compare at least three model families using the same split and metric plan.

skills.title: Bias, variance and regularization
skills.description: Diagnose underfitting and overfitting from learning evidence and control complexity without test-set tuning.
outcomes: Interpret learning or validation results and justify a regularization or data response.

modules.title: Unsupervised learning and representation
modules.description: Explore structure without pretending discovered groups are objective truth.

skills.title: Clustering
skills.description: Compare centroid, density and hierarchical concepts and the role of scaling and distance.
outcomes: Apply and critique a clustering result under at least two parameter or representation choices.

skills.title: Dimensionality reduction
skills.description: Use low-dimensional projections for compression or exploration without overinterpreting visual separation.
outcomes: Apply a linear projection and explain information retained and lost.

skills.title: Anomaly detection
skills.description: Define unusual relative to a reference population and evaluate rare-event alerts with review costs.
outcomes: Design and evaluate a simple anomaly detector without assuming every outlier is harmful.

skills.title: Recommendation concepts
skills.description: Distinguish content, collaborative and popularity baselines and identify feedback-loop and exposure bias.
outcomes: Specify and critique a small recommender objective, baseline and offline evaluation.

modules.title: Neural networks and learned representations
modules.description: Understand layered differentiable models, training signals and architecture roles.

skills.title: Units, layers and activations
skills.description: Trace weighted sums, nonlinear activations and composed representations through a small network.
outcomes: Compute and visualize a forward pass through a tiny feed-forward network.

skills.title: Loss, gradients and optimization
skills.description: Explain how examples, loss and gradient-based updates change parameters without requiring full calculus derivations.
outcomes: Trace a simple parameter update and diagnose learning-rate or scale problems.

skills.title: Validation, regularization and reproducibility
skills.description: Use validation, early stopping, regularization and seeded experiments while reporting variability.
outcomes: Design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage.

skills.title: Convolution, sequence and attention roles
skills.description: Recognize how locality, recurrence and attention encode different structure and compute trade-offs.
outcomes: Match a data structure and task to an architecture family and state limitations.

modules.title: Language, perception and embodied applications
modules.description: Connect representations and models to major application domains and their measurement limits.

skills.title: Natural-language processing
skills.description: Distinguish tokenization, representation, classification, extraction, translation and generation tasks.
outcomes: Frame and evaluate a small NLP task while identifying language and dialect coverage gaps.

skills.title: Computer vision and perception
skills.description: Relate pixels, features, detection, classification and segmentation to sensing conditions and label policy.
outcomes: Design an evaluation plan that includes lighting, viewpoint and subgroup conditions.

skills.title: Robotics and embodied agents
skills.description: Connect sensing, localization, planning, control and actuation under physical safety constraints.
outcomes: Map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior.

skills.title: Multimodal systems
skills.description: Combine modalities while recognizing alignment, missing-modality, provenance and cross-modal error risks.
outcomes: Review a multimodal application and specify modality-specific and joint evaluations.

modules.title: Generative AI, LLMs and retrieval
modules.description: Use generative systems as probabilistic components with grounding, validation and security boundaries.

skills.title: Tokens, next-token modeling and context
skills.description: Explain LLM generation as conditional token prediction with finite context rather than database retrieval or understanding proof.
outcomes: Trace a toy token-generation example and identify unsupported capability claims.

skills.title: Prompts and constrained outputs
skills.description: Specify task, context, constraints and output schema while treating generated output as untrusted.
outcomes: Design and test a prompt-plus-validator workflow against normal and adversarial cases.

skills.title: Embeddings and retrieval augmentation
skills.description: Retrieve relevant versioned passages, preserve provenance and distinguish retrieval failure from generation failure.
outcomes: Build or simulate a small RAG pipeline and evaluate retrieval and answer grounding separately.

skills.title: API, tool and prompt-injection boundaries
skills.description: Keep keys server-side, minimize transmitted data, validate tool arguments and separate untrusted content from instructions.
outcomes: Threat-model and test a mocked LLM API or tool call without exposing secrets or granting autonomous authority.

modules.title: Evaluation and responsible AI
modules.description: Measure performance and harms in context and manage risk across the lifecycle.

skills.title: Metrics, thresholds and baselines
skills.description: Choose task and decision metrics, compare simple baselines and report uncertainty rather than one headline score.
outcomes: Create and justify an evaluation table for regression, classification or generation.

skills.title: Robustness, drift and adversarial cases
skills.description: Evaluate distribution shifts, malformed inputs, rare cases, abuse and changing performance after deployment.
outcomes: Design and execute a stress-test matrix and specify monitoring triggers.

skills.title: Fairness and explainability
skills.description: Measure relevant group outcomes, examine proxy features and match explanation method to stakeholder and decision.
outcomes: Audit a model slice, identify an unresolved fairness trade-off and communicate explanation limits.

skills.title: Govern, map, measure and manage
skills.description: Assign ownership, document context, measure prioritized risks and implement proportionate controls and incident response.
outcomes: Produce a concise AI risk register with owners, evidence, controls, residual risk and stop criteria.

modules.title: Reproducible AI capstone
modules.description: Integrate problem framing, modeling, evaluation and governance into a bounded project.

skills.title: Project card and experiment plan
skills.description: Document users, decision context, data, baseline, metrics, risks, non-goals and acceptance or stop criteria.
outcomes: Produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting.

skills.title: Reproducible pipeline
skills.description: Pin environment and data version, seed stochastic components and separate fit, evaluation and reporting steps.
outcomes: Reproduce a recorded experiment from a clean environment and explain remaining nondeterminism.

skills.title: Model card and human oversight
skills.description: Report intended use, exclusions, data, metrics, slices, limitations, review points and escalation paths.
outcomes: Write a model card whose claims match recorded evaluation evidence.

skills.title: AI foundations capstone
skills.description: Implement a modest symbolic, learned or retrieval-grounded system and evaluate it against baseline, stress and risk criteria.
outcomes: Deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability.

exit_outcomes: Characterize AI problems and implement modest search, reasoning, planning or machine-learning solutions.
Build leakage-resistant data and evaluation workflows with meaningful baselines, metrics and stress tests.
Explain neural, language, perception and generative systems at a warranted conceptual level without anthropomorphic claims.
Use prompting, retrieval and model APIs through validation, provenance, secret and prompt-injection controls.
Deliver a reproducible AI project with documented intended use, limits, human oversight and residual risks.
